articles.title: BIG IDEAS IN 2024
articles.summary: Smart energy grids, voice-first companion apps, programmable medicines, AI tools for kids, and the next Disney being a gaming company. a16z asked over 40 partners to preview one big idea they believe will drive innovation in 2024.

articles.title: SOFT POWER IN TECH
articles.summary: Collective human activity is often the product of storytelling. A handful of investors and companies within tech have mastered the art of storytelling, leveraging brand and soft influence as a way to get people to work for them, invest in them, and believe in them. This article explores how soft power works, and provides some in-depth examples of companies doing it well.

articles.title: ELON MUSK'S X.AI AIMS TO RAISE $1 BILLION
articles.summary: Are we even remotely surprised at this point?

articles.title: METRIC TO KNOW BEFORE YOUR NEXT INVESTOR MEETING
articles.summary: Getting caught out and not knowing something important when an investor asks is an awkward feeling. Here’s a checklist of the things you should have top of mind before you do your regular sync with an existing investor.

articles.title: TIPS FOR BUILDING A SUCCESSFUL HARDWARE COMPANY
articles.summary: Software has a lot of playbooks by now, hardware doesn’t. Increasingly, investors look to hardware in the climate space, and even software companies view it as a way to build a moat and extend their activity into the real world. Here are some thoughts on how a founder can position themselves to build a successful hardware company.

articles.title: YOU’VE SEEN ONE, YOU’VE SEEN ONE
articles.summary: It’s easy to get complacent and jaded in the face of so many seen-one-seen-’em-all companies/people/products. This post gives a pretty good formula for how to make sure you’re seeing the forest for the trees.

articles.title: &FACTS (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: AI-powered product research for DTC brands.

articles.title: VIZZLY (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Customer-facing analytics for modern SaaS companies.

articles.title: THE PITFALLS OF PATTERN RECOGNITION
articles.summary: “We tried that before”. Everyone knows that phrase at a startup, right? It’s true - if you hear hooves it’s likely a horse - but sometimes it’s a zebra. Relying on pattern recognition to make decisions can only get you so far. Here are some tactics on how to make sure you’re not wrongly relying on defaults.

articles.title: VERTICAL AI
articles.summary: Horizontal software, software designed to solve a user's pain regardless of the type of user that feels it, has defined the last 10 years of SaaS. Vertical SaaS, by contrast, is designed for a single or narrow user set, enabling the solution to be tailored to their needs. In this article, a Greylock partner forecasts that moving forward, users’ expectations around tailored services will govern how we build software.

articles.title: WHY STABILITY AI IS LAUNCHING A SUBSCRIPTION FEE
WHEN SHOULD I EXPECT A NEW SALES HIRE TO BE ON TARGET?
articles.summary: With some variance depending on product complexity, it should still be within 1.5 sales cycles.

articles.title: IN LIKE A LAMB, OUT LIKE A LION
articles.summary: Large software companies like Datadog and Mongo are growing at 30% YoY. Looking back on previous years, this might not seem stellar, but in the current market, that’s top percentile. Many feel the sentiment in the room is that this signals a solid year ahead for software.

articles.title: NEW YEAR, NEW COMPANY - 10 ADDITIONAL SPOTS IN W24 FOR LATE APPLICANTS
articles.summary: If you’ve got an idea and a team, now might be the time to take advantage of YC opening up 10 more spots for W24 class. Now is the PERFECT time to build something.
